summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates host information over HTTP
details: On install, postinstall.js loads the os, http, and child_process modules and issues outbound HTTP requests carrying host identifiers (os.hostname()) collected from the installer's machine. The script runs automatically as a postinstall lifecycle hook on `npm install`, with no relation to any documented purpose of the package. This is a classic install-time host-reconnaissance / exfiltration pattern: any developer or build system that installs this package will leak host data to the hardcoded remote endpoint without user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
